TR. 5(l5)/94-T^^] femm.—^ 1w? ^T. ft. s?r. 324 Tnfrsr 14-2-1956 wi v3Tf«Rjf^RT ft^ "T^ ^ 3lfa <T«NV^I<'Hr. 3»T. ft. 870(3?) <TT&£ 8-7-97 s f a m ^ . ft. 17 (3T), tfrfter 5 ^FTCft, 2000 5TO *f?ftftm fsfrqr TT(T | 875 GI/2000 MINISTRY OF LAW, JUSTICE & COMPANY AFFAIRS (Department of Legal Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th March, 2000 G.S.R. 262(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 15 of the Notaries Act, 1952 (53 of 1952), the Central Government hereby makes the following Rules further to amend the Notaries Rules, 1956, namely:— 1. These Rules may be called the Notaries (Second Amendment) Rules, 2000. 2. In the Notaries Rules, 1956,— (a) in rule 8, in sub-rule (4), for the words "three years", the words "five years" shall be substituted; (b) in rule 8A, for the words "three years", the woTds "five years" shall be substituted. [F. No. 5 (15)/94-NC] KRISHNA KUMAR, Jt. Secy.& Legal Adviser Note:-The Principal Rules, were notfied vide S.R.0.324, dated 14-2-1956 and subsequently amended by GSR 870 (E) dated 8-7-1997 and G.S.R. 17 (E) dated 5th January, 2000.
